scenario: Vertex Insurance, an insurer processing 12000 claims per day, starts a new custom outbound connector project for Camunda 8.8 using Maven. A developer wants to add the Connector SDK dependency manually instead of cloning the GitHub template, so the team can keep its existing repository layout untouched while still implementing OutboundConnectorFunction.
question: Which Maven coordinates does the Connector SDK documentation specify for the core dependency?

options.text: groupId io.camunda.zeebe, artifactId zeebe-connector-sdk, version ${version.zeebe}, declared under a runtime scope so it ships with the Zeebe broker at deploy time
groupId io.camunda, artifactId camunda-connector-runtime, version ${version.connectors}, declared as a parent BOM import rather than as a normal dependency entry
groupId org.camunda.bpm, artifactId camunda-connect-core, version ${version.camunda}, with the connect-plugins module added separately to enable the SDK annotations
groupId io.camunda.connector, artifactId connector-core, version ${version.connectors}, declared as a normal Maven dependency in the project pom.xml file

a.text: Incorrect. The SDK is not published under io.camunda.zeebe and is not a runtime artifact shipped with the broker; the documented coordinates use the connector groupId and artifact connector-core.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs show a regular dependency, not a BOM import, and the artifactId is connector-core rather than camunda-connector-runtime.

c.text: Incorrect. camunda-connect-core belongs to the legacy Camunda 7 Connect framework; the Camunda 8.8 Connector SDK uses io.camunda.connector:connector-core.

d.text: Correct. The Connector SDK setup documentation lists a Maven dependency with groupId io.camunda.connector, artifactId connector-core, and version ${version.connectors}, added directly to the project pom.xml.

explanation: To set up a Connector SDK project manually, add the io.camunda.connector:connector-core dependency at version ${version.connectors} to your Maven pom.xml (or the equivalent Gradle implementation line). The templates on GitHub use the same coordinates.
